title: Bryan Elsley

infobox.value: [Jamie Brittain](/wiki/Jamie_Brittain "Jamie Brittain") [Jess Brittain](/wiki/Jess_Brittain "Jess Brittain") [Edith Elsley](/w/index.php?title=Edith_Elsley&action=edit&redlink=1 "Edith Elsley (page does not exist)") [Nommie Elsley](/w/index.php?title=Nommie_Elsley&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nommie Elsley (page does not exist)")

from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Bryan Elsley"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Bryan+Elsley%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(born 17 May 1961 in [Dalkeith](/wiki/Dalkeith "Dalkeith"), [Midlothian](/wiki/Midlothian "Midlothian"))[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] is a Scottish [television writer](/wiki/Television_writer "Television writer"), best known for the co-creation of [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen [drama](/wiki/Television_series "Television series") *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* with his son, [Jamie Brittain](/wiki/Jamie_Brittain "Jamie Brittain"). Other television dramas include *[Rose and Maloney](/wiki/Rose_and_Maloney "Rose and Maloney")*, *[The Young Person's Guide to Becoming a Rock Star](/wiki/The_Young_Person%27s_Guide_to_Becoming_a_Rock_Star "The Young Person's Guide to Becoming a Rock Star")*, *[The Crow Road](/wiki/The_Crow_Road_(TV_series) "The Crow Road (TV series)")*, *[Dates](/wiki/Dates_(TV_series) "Dates (TV series)")*, and *[Kiss Me First](/wiki/Kiss_Me_First_(TV_series) "Kiss Me First (TV series)")*.

Edwards, Gareth (20 January 2007). ["Bryan's had to Bare all in *Skins*"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080118134626/http://living.scotsman.com/ViewArticle.aspx?articleid=3339588). *Edinburgh Evening News*. Johnston Press. Archived from [the original](http://living.scotsman.com/ViewArticle.aspx?articleid=3339588) on 18 January 2008. Retrieved 31 December 2007.
["Nature Boy (1999)"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/cumbria/content/articles/2006/03/29/cumbriaonfilm_nature_boy_feature.shtml). *Cumbria on film*. BBC Cumbria. 23 October 2007. Retrieved 31 December 2007.

["The Crow Road box set review: a masterful adaptation of Iain Banks's addictive family saga"](https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2015/apr/23/crow-road-iain-banks-masterful-adaptation). *The Guardian*. 23 April 2015.

["Bryan Elsley"](https://web.archive.org/web/20190503213544/http://www.bfi.org.uk/films-tv-people/4ce2ba84d759f). British Film Institute. Archived from [the original](https://www2.bfi.org.uk/films-tv-people/4ce2ba84d759f) on 3 May 2019.

Saracevic, Ena (1 September 2025). ["Counsels filming: BBC Scotland's new Glasgow-set legal drama from the creator of Skins confirms cast"](https://www.scotsman.com/arts-and-culture/film-and-tv/casting-glasgow-bbc-counsels-filming-5296896). *The Scotsman*. Retrieved 1 September 2025.

Whittock, Jesse (31 August 2025). ["BBC Legal Drama 'Counsels' Casts Its Hotshot Lawyer Leads As Cameras Roll"](https://deadline.com/2025/08/bbc-legal-drama-counsels-leads-cast-filming-starts-scotland-1236502366/). *Deadline Hollywood*. Retrieved 1 September 2025.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Scottish television writer

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This [biography of a living person](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") **needs additional [citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help by adding [reliable sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources"). [Contentious material](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons#Remove_contentious_material_that_is_unsourced_or_poorly_sourced "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") about living persons that is unsourced or [poorly sourced](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources#Questionable_and_self-published_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources") **must be removed immediately** from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Bryan Elsley"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Bryan+Elsley%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Bryan+Elsley%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Bryan Elsley | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1961-05-17) 17 May 1961 (age 64)  [Dalkeith](/wiki/Dalkeith "Dalkeith"), [Midlothian](/wiki/Midlothian "Midlothian"), Scotland |
| Occupation | Television writer |
| Children | [Jamie Brittain](/wiki/Jamie_Brittain "Jamie Brittain") [Jess Brittain](/wiki/Jess_Brittain "Jess Brittain") [Edith Elsley](/w/index.php?title=Edith_Elsley&action=edit&redlink=1 "Edith Elsley (page does not exist)") [Nommie Elsley](/w/index.php?title=Nommie_Elsley&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nommie Elsley (page does not exist)") |

**Bryan Elsley** (born 17 May 1961 in [Dalkeith](/wiki/Dalkeith "Dalkeith"), [Midlothian](/wiki/Midlothian "Midlothian"))[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] is a Scottish [television writer](/wiki/Television_writer "Television writer"), best known for the co-creation of [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen [drama](/wiki/Television_series "Television series") *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* with his son, [Jamie Brittain](/wiki/Jamie_Brittain "Jamie Brittain"). Other television dramas include *[Rose and Maloney](/wiki/Rose_and_Maloney "Rose and Maloney")*, *[The Young Person's Guide to Becoming a Rock Star](/wiki/The_Young_Person%27s_Guide_to_Becoming_a_Rock_Star "The Young Person's Guide to Becoming a Rock Star")*, *[The Crow Road](/wiki/The_Crow_Road_(TV_series) "The Crow Road (TV series)")*, *[Dates](/wiki/Dates_(TV_series) "Dates (TV series)")*, and *[Kiss Me First](/wiki/Kiss_Me_First_(TV_series) "Kiss Me First (TV series)")*.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bryan_Elsley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Elsley attended [Dalkeith High School](/wiki/Dalkeith_High_School "Dalkeith High School") before going on to read English and History at the [Alcuin College](/wiki/Alcuin_College "Alcuin College"), [University of York](/wiki/University_of_York "University of York") in [York](/wiki/York "York"), [England](/wiki/England "England"), where he graduated with a B.A. in 1982.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bryan_Elsley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

While a student at the University of York, Elsley met and collaborated with [Harry Enfield](/wiki/Harry_Enfield "Harry Enfield").[[1]](#cite_note-1) They created a [comedy duo](/wiki/Comedy_duo "Comedy duo"), "Dusty and Dick", and performed a sell out show at the [Edinburgh Festival Fringe](/wiki/Edinburgh_Festival_Fringe "Edinburgh Festival Fringe").

Elsley took up a career in theatrical writing, and then pursued television writing after parting from Enfield. For three years, Elsley was artistic director of Pocket Theatre Cumbria, which was based at [Kendal](/wiki/Kendal "Kendal")'s [Brewery Arts Centre](/wiki/Brewery_Arts_Centre "Brewery Arts Centre").[[2]](#cite_note-2) At that time he was living at [Staveley](/wiki/Staveley,_Cumbria "Staveley, Cumbria"), near Kendal,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and also writing episodes for the TV series *[Casualty](/wiki/Casualty_(TV_series) "Casualty (TV series)")* and *[London's Burning](/wiki/London%27s_Burning_(TV_series) "London's Burning (TV series)")*. These, along with his short film *Govan Ghost Story* (1989), opened up opportunities for other television writing. Elsley's big break came when he was commissioned by the BBC to adapt [Iain Banks](/wiki/Iain_Banks "Iain Banks")'s novel *The Crow Road* for television.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

On 18 March 2010, Elsey announced via the *Skins* blog that the final episode of series 4 was his last as a writer for the UK series, although Elsley did return to write the opening episode for series 6 in 2012. He executive produced *[Skins U.S.](/wiki/Skins_(American_TV_series) "Skins (American TV series)")* in 2011.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

His series *[Counsels](/wiki/Counsels_(TV_series) "Counsels (TV series)")* about a group of young lawyers in Scotland was commissioned by the BBC in 2025, with filming taking place that year in Glasgow.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bryan_Elsley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Elsley has five children and currently lives in [Kentish Town](/wiki/Kentish_Town "Kentish Town"), [London](/wiki/London "London").

## Film and television work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bryan_Elsley&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Film and television work")]

* *[The Crow Road](/wiki/The_Crow_Road_(TV_series) "The Crow Road (TV series)")* (1996)
* *[The Young Person's Guide to Becoming a Rock Star](/wiki/The_Young_Person%27s_Guide_to_Becoming_a_Rock_Star "The Young Person's Guide to Becoming a Rock Star")* (1998)
* *[Complicity](/wiki/Complicity_(film) "Complicity (film)")* (2000)
* *[Rose and Maloney](/wiki/Rose_and_Maloney "Rose and Maloney")* (2002–2005)
* *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007–2013)
* *[Skins U.S.](/wiki/Skins_(American_TV_series) "Skins (American TV series)")* (2011)
* *[Dates](/wiki/Dates_(TV_series) "Dates (TV series)")* (2013)
* *[Clique](/wiki/Clique_(TV_series) "Clique (TV series)")* (2016)
* *[Kiss Me First](/wiki/Kiss_Me_First_(TV_series) "Kiss Me First (TV series)")* (2018)
